steps.action: The schottische (German for 'Scottish') is a par with weight transfer through footwork, hold hands, grounded.
steps.text: The schottische (German for 'Scottish') is a par with weight transfer through footwork, hold hands, grounded.

steps.action: The schottische is considered by The Oxford Comp
steps.text: The schottische is considered by The Oxford Companion to Music to be a kind of slower polka, with continental-European origin.

steps.action: The schottische basic step is made up of two sid
steps.text: The schottische basic step is made up of two sidesteps to the left and right, followed by a turn in four steps.

steps.action: In some countries, the sidesteps and turn are re
steps.text: In some countries, the sidesteps and turn are replaced by strathspey hopping steps.

steps.action: All of these rhythms are typically danced in pai
steps.text: All of these rhythms are typically danced in pairs, being xote the slower and simpler style of dancing, in which the couple alternate left-left-hop-right-right-hop steps.

steps.action: It is danced in its more traditional folk varian
steps.text: It is danced in its more traditional folk variants as an exhibition dance, and also in the balfolk scene.

steps.action: It rapidly disseminated from the salons to the c
steps.text: It rapidly disseminated from the salons to the countryside, becoming one of the most popular folk dances.

steps.action: It is one of the most common dance found in balf
steps.text: It is one of the most common dance found in balfolk.

steps.action: The schottish Espagnol or Spanish schottische, a
steps.text: The schottish Espagnol or Spanish schottische, also known as the "seven step" gained popularity in France in the early 1900s.

steps.action: Also known as a "barn dance", the "German" is a
steps.text: Also known as a "barn dance", the "German" is a two-hand dance played to tunes that end each eight-bar phrase with three lightly accented quarter notes.

steps.action: In the beginning it was just a high-class ballro
steps.text: In the beginning it was just a high-class ballroom dance.

steps.action: It became popular after a while and then a more
steps.text: It became popular after a while and then a more "rustic" style was created for public parties and dance halls.

steps.action: The "Chotis" is now part of contemporary Philipp
steps.text: The "Chotis" is now part of contemporary Philippine folk dance repertoire.

steps.action: Notable local variations of the dance include Ch
steps.text: Notable local variations of the dance include Chotis Dingreña from the Ilocos region, and Escotis from the Aklan and Capiz provinces in Panay Island.

steps.action: It enjoyed widespread popularity in the late 19t
steps.text: It enjoyed widespread popularity in the late 19th century, although its practice declined in the following centuries and its use is marginal or virtually non-existent outside traditional dance academies.

steps.action: The pairs in groups of four, six or eight, form
steps.text: The pairs in groups of four, six or eight, form a circle and dance embraced all together.

steps.action: The circle starts to rotate until a moment when
steps.text: The circle starts to rotate until a moment when the pairs pass; that is, the pairs that are opposite each other switch places crossing each other in the center of the circle.

steps.action: After everybody made their pass, they continue t
steps.text: After everybody made their pass, they continue to dance by rotating in circle.

steps.action: It has two main forms in 24 beat, one being more
steps.text: It has two main forms in 24 beat, one being more popular than the other.

steps.action: Both versions are similar in starting line-up to
steps.text: Both versions are similar in starting line-up to the Gay Gordons and has a polka feel to it.

steps.action: On the first two main beats, each partner raises
steps.text: On the first two main beats, each partner raises onto their toes and performs a Highland step, bringing back the heel of extended foot against the calf of the other (inside) leg, whilst hopping on the other foot.

steps.action: They then trot forward 4 steps to the beat, pivo
steps.text: They then trot forward 4 steps to the beat, pivot quickly so that the man is on the right and woman on the left, both facing the opposite direction of travel.

steps.action: They perform the same Highland steps as before b
steps.text: They perform the same Highland steps as before but now on the opposite foot, before trotting forward 4 steps again.

steps.action: They now trot sideways 3 steps to the left (man
steps.text: They now trot sideways 3 steps to the left (man left/right/left; woman right/left/right), then hop on the same foot as the third step, then trot sideways right (man right/left/right; woman left/right/left) and hop.
